contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 75
- Description: Sophia self-exec + sudo on her own box, host-location awareness, Grok key, automated weekly AMI backups
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_autopilot/pull/114
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/Cypher-Defense/pull/37
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/Cypher-Defense/pull/38
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/340
  
  Details:
  Resolved Sophia's inability to run sudo/install packages on her own box and three related infrastructure gaps. Root cause was not sudo (ubuntu already has passwordless sudo) but reachability: the ssh_run FLEET registry omitted the autopilot's own box and sophia_infra.pub was absent from its own authorized_keys. truesight_autopilot#114: added an 'autopilot' self-host on 127.0.0.1 loopback (survives IP changes / blue-green), self-trust step in deploy.sh + user-data.sh, tesseract-ocr/poppler-utils on future boxes, and a live host-identity block in the system prompt (IMDSv2 instance id/type/region/IPs, local fallback, 4 unit tests) so Sophia stops hallucinating which machine she is on. Applied live: verified self-ssh + sudo root + tesseract 4.1.1 installed via the self-exec path, host-identity block shows i-02c699d3d7efbdc82/t3.medium. Granted the Grok API key on the box (.env + restart; /health grok_key_set=true). Automated weekly AMI backups as a Cypher-Defense GitHub Action (#37, ASCII-description fix #38): resolves the instance by Name tag (robust to resizes/blue-green), create-image --no-reboot, retain newest 8 with snapshot pruning, repo AWS secrets set; first validated AMI ami-0dae91c5216989753. Updated AWS_DIGITAL_INFRASTRUCTURE §4.5 (weekly-AMI TODO done) and CONTEXT_UPDATES for the parallel infra session.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 125.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
